title: "You'll need journalism so distinctive it has its own gravity": A.G. Sulzberger on how news organizations can stand up to AI companies
tldr: The NYT publisher's WAN-IFRA keynote argues that distinctiveness is the only defense against AI commoditization of news. A meaty think-piece on the future of journalism and content value.
why_fresh: sharp framing of distinctiveness as moat against AI
angle: What 'gravity' means for brand content: why distinctive corporate narrative beats AI-generated sameness.

title: Your Content Isn't Just Competing With Other Brands Anymore
tldr: In AI-driven discovery, content competes against AI answers and synthesis, not just rival brands. Reframes the entire SEO/share-of-voice playbook.
why_fresh: new competitive frame for AI search era
angle: How to audit your content pillars for an AI-answer world where the summary is the competitor.

title: The #1 Role Your Content Team Needs in 2026 Is a Managing Editor
tldr: As AI makes production cheap, editorial judgment becomes the differentiator — making the managing editor the key hire. A clear thesis on where human value sits.
why_fresh: role shift driven by AI economics
angle: Why editorial judgment is the last defensible content skill — and how to structure a team around it.

title: 'Social media is now three distinct categories'
tldr: Researchers argue platforms have split into broadcasters, micro-communities, and AI-driven active experiences. A useful new mental model for social strategy.
angle: Rebuilding your social strategy around three platform types instead of one undifferentiated feed.

title: Coca-Cola's AI-powered Jos\u00e9 Mourinho campaign could signal a shift in celebrity partnerships
tldr: Coke's series is hosted by Mourinho's AI clone rather than the man himself, hinting at a structural change in how brands license talent. Concrete signal of synthetic media going mainstream.
angle: When the spokesperson is synthetic: governance and trust questions for AI-cloned talent in brand content.

title: YouTube's AI remix push exposes a looming reckoning for the creator economy
tldr: YouTube's Gemini Omni integration surfaces deep IP and consent problems for generative AI in creator workflows. A trend story with real stakes.
why_fresh: platform AI feature triggers creator IP reckoning
angle: Who owns remixed content? Why creator-economy IP norms are about to break.

title: How to prepare for more declines in traditional Google search traffic
tldr: With AI Overviews shrinking clicks, experts share tactics for maintaining visibility when AI supplies the answers. Practical for any owned-media strategy.
angle: A content-decay audit framework for the zero-click search era.

title: Layoffs don't have to feel inhumane
tldr: Argues layoffs are a trust moment, not a messaging problem, and that employees judge leadership almost immediately. Strong internal-comms and change-management angle.
why_fresh: reframes layoff comms as trust event
angle: Layoff communications as the ultimate test of internal trust — a playbook for change-management messaging.

title: 3 questions to ask before putting your CEO in front of the media during a crisis
tldr: FleishmanHillard's Elizabeth Cook warns a crisis is not the time to train a weak CEO. Practical executive-positioning and crisis guidance.
angle: A pre-crisis CEO readiness checklist that separates spokesperson-ready from media-liability execs.

title: Why 'AI Productivity Gains' Is the Wrong Pitch for Every Stakeholder Above You
tldr: Pitching AI as productivity won't move CMOs, CFOs or legal — reframe the metric per audience to unlock budget. Smart on internal persuasion and metrics.
angle: Translating AI value into the language of each C-suite stakeholder to defend content headcount.

title: Understanding the 3 categories of online chatter
tldr: Opening Ragan session on how news now emerges bottom-up from comments and posts, with a framework for categorizing online chatter. Useful for monitoring and issue management.
angle: Building an issue-management radar around three types of online chatter.

title: The best way to pitch Business Insider, direct from their editors
tldr: BI newsroom leaders reveal what gets executives featured, from first-person POV to their outfit. Tactical media-relations intel.
angle: What editor preferences reveal about the shift toward first-person executive storytelling in earned media.

title: Podcasts and influencers least culturally relevant media
tldr: Thinkbox study finds TV still drives the largest share of cultural influence while podcasts and creators rank lowest. A counter-narrative worth interrogating.
why_fresh: contrarian data on creator cultural weight
angle: Cultural relevance vs reach: why the podcast/creator hype may be outrunning its actual influence.

title: How a veteran video games journalist went solo and built a sustainable business
tldr: Stephen Totilo's move from staff to solo, profitable operation is a case study in independent media business models. Relevant to creator-economy and journalism economics.
angle: What solo journalists' business models teach brands about audience-first content.

title: AI 'content creators' are getting harder to spot
tldr: The Verge tracks how AI influencers have become increasingly indistinguishable from real ones, raising disclosure and trust questions. Relevant synthetic-media trend.
angle: The coming disclosure crisis: how brands should handle audiences who can't tell AI creators from humans.
